content:
Hi, I am currently learning to write linux kernel modules in rust. Are there any suitable modules waiting to be rewritten in Rust? And is there already a good use case for rewriting linux kernel modules in Rust?
By the way, I noticed an issue on github about a plan to rewrite tty0tty in rust(https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/62). Is there any progress on that?
By the way, are there any popular tools for migrating from an original C module to a new Rust module? Or do we now rely purely on developer experience to implement module rewriting?

hello wenzhao
i am not in a position to give advice (i think i am on the same path as you a bit).
i've been working on an i2c-stub and taking notes as i go.
there may not yet be a tool for migrating a c module to rust but i think documenting your experience will be valuable for others.
whatever you choose to work on, i will be very interested to read about it.
I keep a very informal list of project ideas at my patch registry https://github.com/tgross35/RFL-patch-registry/?tab=readme-ov-file#project-idea-board
